# Introduction to Zechariah

## Part 1: General Introduction

### Outline of the Book of Zechariah

1. The eight night visions ([1:7вҖ“6:8](../01/07.md))
    * Yahweh sends his messengers throughout the whole earth ([1:7вҖ“17](../01/07.md))
    * Four horns and four craftsmen ([1:18вҖ“21](../01/18.md))
    * The measuring line ([2:1вҖ“13](../02/01.md))
    * The priest in filthy clothes ([3:1вҖ“10](../03/01.md))
    * The golden lampstand olive trees ([4:1вҖ“14](../04/01.md))
    * The flying scroll ([5:1вҖ“4](../05/01.md))
    * The woman in a basket ([5:5вҖ“11](../05/05.md))
    * Four chariots ([6:1вҖ“8](../06/01.md))
1. A crown for the high priest ([6:9вҖ“15](../06/09.md))
1. Questions about fasting ([7:1вҖ“8:23](../07/01.md))
1. Yahweh judges the nations and saves his people ([9:1вҖ“11:17](../09/01.md))
1. Final message ([12:1вҖ“14:21](../12/01.md))
    * Yahweh will finally defeat IsraelвҖҷs enemies ([12:1вҖ“9](../12/01.md))

    * Yahweh purifies Israel and removes false prophets ([13:1вҖ“6](../13/01.md))

### What is the Book of Zechariah about?

Zechariah wrote to the people of Judah who returned from being exiled in Babylon. The people of Judah continued to sin, as their ancestors did before they went into exile. Zechariah called the people to repent and to obey Yahweh. And he called them to renew the covenant with Yahweh. Zechariah also prophesied about the Messiah and about Israel finally being victorious over her enemies. (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/covenant]])

### How should the title of this book be translated?

This book traditionally has been titled вҖңZechariahвҖқ or вҖңThe Book of Zechariah.вҖқ Translators may decide to call it the вҖңThe Book About ZechariahвҖқ or вҖңThe Sayings of Zechariah.вҖқ (See: [[rc://*/ta/man/translate/translate-names]])

### Who wrote the Book of Zechariah?

The prophet Zechariah wrote this book between 520 and 518 B.C.

## Part 2: Important Religious and Cultural Concepts

### Did Zechariah prophesy before Ezra, Nehemiah, and Haggai?

Zechariah prophesied before the time of Ezra and Nehemiah. He prophesied at the same time as Haggai and continued after Haggai.

## Part 3: Important Translation Issues

### What is the meaning of the term вҖңIsraelвҖқ?

The name вҖңIsraelвҖқ is used in many different ways in the Bible. Jacob was a son of Isaac. God changed JacobвҖҷs name to Israel. The descendants of Jacob became a nation also called Israel. Eventually, the nation of Israel split into two kingdoms. The northern kingdom was named Israel. The southern kingdom was named Judah. Zechariah uses the term вҖңIsraelвҖқ to refer to both the nation of Israel and the northern kingdom of Israel.

### What imagery does Zechariah use in reference to the Messiah?

Zechariah presented the Messiah as both a king and a shepherd. Zechariah said that the Messiah would come and deliver his people. However, the Messiah would also be pierced ([12:10](../12/10.md)) and struck with the sword ([13:7](../13/07.md)). (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/christ]])

### What does the phrase вҖңthat dayвҖқ mean?

Zechariah often spoke of the last days by using the phrase вҖңthat dayвҖқ or вҖңin that day.вҖқ When referring to a future вҖңday,вҖқ the translator should be aware that the author may be speaking about the last days. (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/lastday]])

# Zechariah 1 General Notes

## Structure and Formatting

This chapter is written in prose mainly with imagery used throughout to portray ZechariahвҖҷs vision given by the Lord.

Some translations prefer to set apart quotations. The ULT and many other English translations set lines 1:3вҖ“6, 14вҖ“17, which are extended quotations, farther to the right on the page than the rest of the text.

## Religious and Cultural Concepts in This Chapter

### Visions

This book contains a number of visions given to Zechariah, so the images are not ones Zechariah actually saw but were given to him in a vision or dream. It is important for each of these visions to keep their meanings and not be interpreted to mean any specific thing.

## Translation Issues in This Chapter

### Date

Zechariah uses two separate dates in this first chapter. When compared to the book of Haggai, ZechariahвҖҷs prophecy was proclaimed just a few weeks after Haggai. However, they prophesied in two different places: Haggai was in Jerusalem and Zechariah was somewhere outside of Jerusalem. (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/prophet]])

Zechariah is using **the word of Yahweh came to Zechariah** as a common expression of the culture to mean вҖңYahweh communicated a message to Zechariah.вҖқ If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [Yahweh spoke his message to Zechariah] or [Yahweh gave his message to Zechariah]

Alternate translation: [are these craftsmen вҖҰ These horns are the nations вҖҰ And these craftsmen have come]

Quote: Ч•Ц·вҒ ЧҷЦјЦёЧ‘Ц№ЦӨЧҗЧ•Цј ЧҗЦөЦЁЧңЦјЦ¶Ч”Цҷ ЧңЦ°вҒ Ч”Ц·Ч—ЦІЧЁЦҙЦЈЧҷЧ“ ЧҗЦ№ЧӘЦёЦ”вҒ Чқ
Note: The term translated as **And** here indicates that what follows is the result or purpose of what came before. The craftsmen have come in order to terrify the nations that scattered Judah. Use a connector in your language that makes this clear. Alternate translation: [So these have come to terrify them]

Here, **the horns of the nations** represents the military power of those nations. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [the armies of the nations]

Here, **the land of Judah** represents the people who live there. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [against the people of Judah]

The angel is using **according to the mouth of** as a common expression of the culture to mean вҖңso that.вҖқ If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [to the extent that]

The angel is using **has not lifted up his head** as a common expression of the culture to mean вҖңno one was able to resist or act with confidence.вҖқ If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [no one was able to resist them] or [no one dared to oppose them]

Quote: Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч ЦјЦ№Ч©ЧӮЦ°ЧҗЦҙЦҘЧҷЧқ Ч§Ц¶ЦӣЧЁЦ¶Чҹ ЧҗЦ¶ЧңЦҫЧҗЦ¶ЦҘЧЁЦ¶ЧҘ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦјЧ“ЦёЦ–Ч”
Note: Here, **lifting up a horn** represents exercising aggressive military power. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [the ones using their military power against the land of Judah]

# Zechariah 2 General Notes

## Structure and Formatting

This chapter is introduced in prose with imagery used in the rest of the chapter to portray ZechariahвҖҷs third vision about measuring lines given by the Lord.

Some translations prefer to set apart quotations. The ULT and many other English translations set the lines of 2:4вҖ“13, which are extended quotations, farther to the right on the page than the rest of the text.

## Religious and Cultural Concepts in This Chapter

### Visions

This book contains a number of visions given to Zechariah so the images are not ones Zechariah actually saw but were given to him in a vision or dream. It is important for each of these visions to keep their meanings and not be interpreted to mean any specific thing. Please be careful to translate each of the terms in your language.

The expression **I lifted up my eyes** was a common expression meaning вҖңI looked upвҖқ or вҖңI looked intently.вҖқ If this expression does not communicate well in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [I looked up]

The word **behold** here alerts the reader to pay attention to the surprising information that follows. Your language may have a comparable expression that you could use here. Alternate translation: [and there was a man]

Zechariah is using the possessive form to describe a **rope** that is used for **measuring**. If it would be helpful in your language, you could express the idea behind the possessive form with an equivalent phrase. Alternate translation: [a string for measuring]

Quote: Ч•ЦёвҒ ЧҗЦ¶Ч©ЦёЦјЧӮЦҘЧҗ ЧўЦөЧҷЧ Ц·ЦӣвҒ Чҷ Ч•ЦёвҒ ЧҗЦөЦ–ЧЁЦ¶Чҗ Ч•Ц°вҒ Ч”ЦҙЧ ЦөЦјЧ”
Note: The phrase **And I lifted up my eyes and saw, and behold** is a way that Zechariah introduces each new vision in his book. Your language may have its own way of introducing a new vision or important event in a narrative.

The word **behold** here alerts the reader to pay attention to the surprising information that follows. Your language may have a comparable expression that you could use here. Alternate translation: [and suddenly the angel]

The pronoun **him** refers to the angel who had been speaking with Zechariah, not to the man with the measuring line. If it would be helpful in your language, you could state that more clearly. Alternate translation: [to meet the interpreting angel]

Here, **he** refers to the second angel, and **him** refers to the angel who had been speaking with Zechariah. If it would be helpful in your language, you could state that more clearly. Alternate translation: [And the second angel said to the first angel]

The phrase **that young man** likely refers to the man with the measuring line from verse 1. If it would be helpful in your language, you could state that more clearly. Alternate translation: [the young man who was going to measure Jerusalem]

The second angel is referring to all living creatures by naming two broad categories, **man** and **beast**. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [people and animals of every kind]
The word **saying** introduces a quotation. Use a natural form for introducing a quotation in your language.

The second angel is speaking of **Jerusalem** as if it would be like **open areas** without walls, meaning the city will have so many inhabitants that people will overflow beyond its walls. If this image does not communicate well in your language, you could express the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [Jerusalem will be like an unwalled city]
If it would be helpful in your language, you could express the idea behind the abstract noun **multitude** in another way. Alternate translation: [the many people and animals]

Quote: ЧЁЦ»Ц—ЧҘ Ч“Ц·ЦјЧ‘ЦөЦјЦӣЧЁ ЧҗЦ¶ЧңЦҫЧ”Ц·вҒ Ч Ц·ЦјЦҘЧўЦ·ЧЁ Ч”Ц·ЧңЦёЦјЦ–Ч– ЧңЦөвҒ ЧҗЧһЦ№Ц‘ЧЁ
Note: If it would be helpful in your language, you could translate this so that there is not a quotation within a quotation. For example, you could express the inner quotation as an indirect quotation.

Here, the pronoun **I** is repeated for emphasis. Yahweh is emphasizing his personal commitment to protect Jerusalem. If it would be helpful in your language, you could express the emphasis in another way. Alternate translation: [And I, even I, will be for her]
If it would be helpful in your language, you could express the idea behind the abstract noun **declaration** in another way. Alternate translation: [Yahweh declares]

Yahweh is speaking of his protection of Jerusalem as if he were **a wall of fire all around** it, meaning he will powerfully protect the city on every side. If this image does not communicate well in your language, you could express the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [a powerful defense all around]

Quote: Ч•ЦјвҒ ЧңЦ°вҒ ЧӣЦёЧ‘Ц–Ч•Ц№Ч“ ЧҗЦ¶ЦҪЧ”Ц°ЧҷЦ¶ЦҘЧ” Ч‘Ц°вҒ ЧӘЧ•Ц№ЧӣЦёЦҪвҒ Ч”Цј
Note: If it would be helpful in your language, you could express the idea behind the abstract noun **glory** in another way. Alternate translation: [to show how glorious I am, I will be in her midst]
Here, **her** refers to Jerusalem. If it would be helpful in your language, you could state that more clearly. Alternate translation: [in the midst of Jerusalem]

The exclamation **Woe! Woe!** is repeated for emphasis to express urgency and warning. If your language has its own way of expressing an urgent warning, you could use it here. Alternate translation: [Listen! Listen!]

Yahweh is using **the land of the north** to refer to Babylon, since the Babylonians came from the north to invade Judah. If it would be helpful in your language, you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [from the land of Babylon]
If it would be helpful in your language, you could express the idea behind the abstract noun **declaration** in another way. Alternate translation: [this is what Yahweh says]

Quote: ЧӣЦ°ЦјвҒ ЧҗЦ·ЧЁЦ°Ч‘Ц·ЦјЦһЧў ЧЁЧ•ЦјЧ—Ц§Ч•Ц№ЧӘ Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч©ЦёЦјЧҒЧһЦ·ЦӣЧҷЦҙЧқ ЧӨЦөЦјЧЁЦ·ЦҘЧ©Ц°ЧӮЧӘЦҙЦјЧҷ ЧҗЦ¶ЧӘЦ°вҒ ЧӣЦ¶Ц–Чқ
Note: Yahweh compares the way he scattered his people to the way **the four winds of the heavens** blow in every direction. If this comparison does not communicate well in your language, you could express the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [I have scattered you to every part of the earth]
The exclamation **Woe** expresses an urgent call. If your language has its own way of expressing an urgent call, you could use it here. Alternate translation: [Listen, Zion!]

Here, **Zion** represents the people who live in Jerusalem. Yahweh is calling the exiled people of Jerusalem to escape from Babylon. If it would be helpful in your language, you could state that plainly. Alternate translation: [you people of Zion]

Yahweh is using **the daughter of Babylon** as a common expression to refer to the people of Babylon. If this expression does not communicate well in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [the people of Babylon]
The phrase **For thus says Yahweh of Armies** introduces an indirect quotation of what Yahweh says. In your translation, use a natural way of introducing indirect quotations in your language.
If your language does not use an abstract noun for the idea of **glory**, you could express the same idea in another way. Alternate translation: [in order to glorify himself, he has sent me]

The pronoun **he** refers to Yahweh, while the pronoun **me** refers to the prophetic speaker. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [Yahweh has sent me, his prophet]

Quote: Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч Ц№ЦјЧ’ЦөЦЈЧўЦ· Ч‘ЦёЦјвҒ ЧӣЦ¶Ц”Чқ Ч Ц№Ч’ЦөЦ–ЧўЦ· Ч‘Ц°ЦјвҒ Ч‘ЦёЧ‘Ц·ЦҘЧӘ ЧўЦөЧҷЧ ЦҪвҒ Ч•Ц№
Note: Yahweh is using **the one touching you is touching the apple of his eye** as a common expression of the culture to mean вҖңharming you is harming what is most precious to him.вҖқ If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [the one who harms you harms what he treasures most]

Quote: Ч”ЦҙЧ Ц°вҒ Ч ЦҙЦЁЧҷ ЧһЦөЧ ЦҙЦӨЧҷЧЈ
Note: Yahweh is using **behold** as a common expression of the culture to get the attention of his listeners and to emphasize what he is about to say. If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [indeed, I am waving] or [look, I am about to wave]

Quote: ЧһЦөЧ ЦҙЦӨЧҷЧЈ ЧҗЦ¶ЧӘЦҫЧҷЦёЧ“ЦҙвҒ ЧҷЦҷ ЧўЦІЧңЦөЧҷвҒ Ч”Ц¶Ц”Чқ
Note: Here, **my hand** represents YahwehвҖҷs power to punish. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [I am about to strike them with my power]

The pronoun **me** refers to the prophetic speaker. This statement is outside YahwehвҖҷs direct speech. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [Yahweh of Armies has sent me, his prophet]

The terms **Sing** and **rejoice** mean similar things. Yahweh is using the two terms together for emphasis. If it would be clearer for your readers, you could express the emphasis with a single phrase. Alternate translation: [Celebrate with great joy]

Yahweh is using **daughter of Zion** as a common expression of the culture to refer to the people of Jerusalem. If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [people of Zion]

Yahweh is using **behold** as a common expression of the culture to get the attention of his listeners and to emphasize what he is about to say. If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [indeed, I am coming] or [look, I am coming]
If your language does not use an abstract noun for the idea of **declaration**, you could express the same idea in another way. Alternate translation: [this is what Yahweh has declared]

Quote: Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧҷЦёЧ“Ц·Ц•ЧўЦ·ЧӘЦ°Цј ЧӣЦҙЦјЧҷЦҫЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЦҘЧ” ЧҰЦ°Ч‘ЦёЧҗЦ–Ч•Ц№ЧӘ Ч©Ц°ЧҒЧңЦёЧ—Ц·ЦҘвҒ Ч ЦҙЧҷ ЧҗЦөЧңЦёЦҪЧҷЦҙвҒ ЧҡЦ°
Note: Here the pronoun **you** is singular and refers to the people of Zion, and the pronoun **me** refers to the prophetic speaker. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [And you, people of Zion, will know that Yahweh of Armies has sent me, his prophet, to you]

Quote: Ч•Ц°вҒ Ч ЦҙЧңЦ°Ч•Ч•ЦјЦ© Ч’Ч•Ц№ЧҷЦҙЦЁЧқ ЧЁЦ·Ч‘ЦјЦҙЦӨЧҷЧқ ЧҗЦ¶ЧңЦҫЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЧ”Цҷ
Note: By **join themselves to Yahweh**, Zechariah means become worshipers of Yahweh. You could indicate this explicitly in your translation if that would be helpful to your readers. Alternate translation: [And people from many nations will worship Yahweh like the Israelites]
If your language does not use an abstract noun for the idea of **holiness**, you could express the same idea in another way. Alternate translation: [the holy land]

Quote: Ч•Ц°вҒ Ч ЦёЧ—Ц·ЦЁЧң & ЧҗЦ¶ЧӘЦҫЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦјЧ“ЦёЧ”Цҷ Ч—Ц¶ЧңЦ°Ч§Ц”вҒ Ч•Ц№
Note: The word translated as **will inherit** means to take formal, permanent possession of something as oneвҖҷs rightful property. It does not imply that a previous owner has died. If the word you use for **will inherit** would give your readers that wrong impression, you could use a more general term. Alternate translation: [will claim Judah as his possession]

Zechariah is using the exclamation **Hush** to express a strong feeling of reverence and awe before Yahweh. There may be an equivalent word or phrase in your language that you can use in your translation to convey this feeling. If not, you could say what Zechariah was feeling. Alternate translation: [Be silent]
Here, **all flesh** represents all living people. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [all people]

Here, **the face of Yahweh** represents the presence of Yahweh. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [from the presence of Yahweh]

Quote: Ч ЦөЧўЦ–Ч•Ц№ЧЁ ЧһЦҙвҒ ЧһЦ°ЦјЧўЦҘЧ•Ц№Чҹ Ч§ЦёЧ“Ц°Ч©ЦҪЧҒвҒ Ч•Ц№
Note: Here Zechariah is speaking of Yahweh as if he were a person who had been sleeping and has now **been roused** to take action. Your language may have a comparable expression that you can use in your translation, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [he has risen to act from his holy dwelling place]

If it would be more natural in your language, you could state **he has been roused** with an active form. The context indicates that Yahweh himself is the agent of this action. Alternate translation: [he has roused himself]
If your language does not use an abstract noun for the idea of **holiness**, you could express the same idea in another way. Alternate translation: [from his holy dwelling place]

# Zechariah 3 Introduction

## Structure and Formatting

This chapter presents ZechariahвҖҷs fourth night vision: a heavenly courtroom scene in which Joshua the [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/priest]] stands before the [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/angel]] of Yahweh while the adversary accuses him (vv.1вҖ“5), followed by a prophetic oracle about вҖңthe BranchвҖқ and the removal of [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/iniquity]] (vv.6вҖ“10).

## Religious and Cultural Concepts in This Chapter

### Clothing as a Symbol of Moral State

Filthy garments represent sin and defilement, while clean garments and a clean turban represent [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/purify|purification]] and restored priestly authority.

## Translation Issues in This Chapter

### Nested Quotation Structure

Verses 7вҖ“10 contain three levels of quotation: the angel of Yahweh quotes Yahweh of Armies, whose speech includes both a conditional promise and a messianic oracle.
Zechariah is using the word translated as **And** to introduce a new event in the story. Use a word, phrase, or other method in your language that is natural for introducing a new event.

The pronoun **he** could refer to 1) Yahweh. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [Yahweh made me see], or 2) the angel that was speaking to Zechariah. Alternate translation: [The angel made me see]

Zechariah is introducing **Joshua the high priest** as a new participant in the story. Your language may have its own way of introducing new participants that you can use in your translation. Alternate translation: [a man named Joshua who served as the high priest]

Quote: ЧўЦ№ЧһЦөЦ•Ч“ ЧңЦҙвҒ ЧӨЦ°Ч ЦөЦ–Чҷ ЧһЦ·ЧңЦ°ЧҗЦ·ЦЈЧҡЦ° ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЦ‘Ч”
Note: Here, **to the face of** represents вҖңin the presence of.вҖқ If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [standing in the presence of the angel of Yahweh]

Zechariah is introducing **the adversary** as a new participant in the story. Your language may have its own way of introducing new participants that you can use in your translation. Alternate translation: [and a being who was an adversary]

Quote: ЧҷЦҙЧ’Ц°ЧўЦ·ЦЁЧЁ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЦӨЧ” Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ ЧҡЦёЦҷ Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч©ЧӮЦјЦёЧҳЦёЦ”Чҹ Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧҷЦҙЧ’Ц°ЧўЦ·ЦӨЧЁ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЧ”Цҷ Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ ЧҡЦёЦ” Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч‘ЦјЦ№Ч—ЦөЦ–ЧЁ Ч‘ЦјЦҙвҒ ЧҷЧЁЦҪЧ•ЦјЧ©ЧҒЦёЧңЦҙЦёЦ‘Чқ
Note: Yahweh is speaking of himself in the third person as **Yahweh**. If it would be helpful in your language, you could translate this in the first person. Alternate translation: [May I, Yahweh, rebuke you, the adversary, and may I, Yahweh who is choosing Jerusalem, rebuke you]

Quote: ЧҷЦҙЧ’Ц°ЧўЦ·ЦЁЧЁ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЦӨЧ” Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ ЧҡЦёЦҷ Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч©ЧӮЦјЦёЧҳЦёЦ”Чҹ Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧҷЦҙЧ’Ц°ЧўЦ·ЦӨЧЁ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЧ”Цҷ Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ ЧҡЦёЦ” Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч‘ЦјЦ№Ч—ЦөЦ–ЧЁ Ч‘ЦјЦҙвҒ ЧҷЧЁЦҪЧ•ЦјЧ©ЧҒЦёЧңЦҙЦёЦ‘Чқ
Note: These two phrases **May Yahweh rebuke you, the adversary** and **may Yahweh who is choosing Jerusalem rebuke you** mean basically the same thing. The second emphasizes the meaning of the first by repeating the same idea with different words. If it would be helpful to your readers, you could combine them and express the emphasis in another way. Alternate translation: [Certainly, may Yahweh rebuke you, the adversary]

Yahweh is speaking as if Joshua were **a firebrand rescued from the fire**. He means that Joshua has been barely saved from destruction. Your language may have a comparable expression that you can use in your translation, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [one who has been barely saved from destruction]

The expression **rescued** is a passive verbal form. If it would be unnatural to use that form, you could express the idea in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [a firebrand that God rescued from the fire]

Quote: ЧҷЦҙЧ’Ц°ЧўЦ·ЦЁЧЁ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЦӨЧ” Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ ЧҡЦёЦҷ Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч©ЧӮЦјЦёЧҳЦёЦ”Чҹ Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧҷЦҙЧ’Ц°ЧўЦ·ЦӨЧЁ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЧ”Цҷ Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ ЧҡЦёЦ” Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч‘ЦјЦ№Ч—ЦөЦ–ЧЁ Ч‘ЦјЦҙвҒ ЧҷЧЁЦҪЧ•ЦјЧ©ЧҒЦёЧңЦҙЦёЦ‘Чқ
Note: Yahweh is expressing what he desires to happen to the adversary. See the discussion in the introduction to this book about this form of speech and follow the decision of your team of how a speaker will express in this context what he wants someone to do. Alternate translation: [I rebuke you, the adversary, and I, Yahweh who is choosing Jerusalem, rebuke you]

Quote: Ч”ЦІвҒ ЧңЦ§Ч•Ц№Чҗ Ч–Ц¶ЦҰЧ” ЧҗЦ–Ч•ЦјЧ“ ЧһЦ»ЧҰЦјЦёЦҘЧң ЧһЦөвҒ ЧҗЦөЦҪЧ©ЧҒ
Note: Yahweh is using the question form to emphasize that Joshua has indeed been rescued from destruction. If a speaker of your language would not use the question form for that purpose, you could translate this as a statement or as an exclamation. Alternate translation: [Surely this is a firebrand rescued from the fire!]

Zechariah is speaking as if JoshuaвҖҷs sinfulness were **filthy garments** that he was wearing. Your language may have a comparable expression that you can use in your translation, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [filthy clothes signifying sin and guilt] or [was deeply sinful]

Quote: Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧўЦ№ЧһЦөЦ–Ч“ ЧңЦҙвҒ ЧӨЦ°Ч ЦөЦҘЧҷ Ч”Ц·вҒ ЧһЦјЦ·ЧңЦ°ЧҗЦёЦҪЧҡЦ°
Note: Here, **to the face of** represents вҖңin the presence of.вҖқ If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [standing in the presence of the angel]

Quote: Ч•ЦҙвҒ ЧҷЧ”Ч•Ц№Ч©ЧҒЦ»Ц•ЧўЦ· Ч”ЦёЧҷЦёЦҘЧ” ЧңЦёЧ‘Ц»Ц–Ч©ЧҒ Ч‘ЦјЦ°Ч’ЦёЧ“ЦҙЦЈЧҷЧқ ЧҰЧ•Ц№ЧҗЦҙЦ‘ЧҷЧқ Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧўЦ№ЧһЦөЦ–Ч“ ЧңЦҙвҒ ЧӨЦ°Ч ЦөЦҘЧҷ Ч”Ц·вҒ ЧһЦјЦ·ЧңЦ°ЧҗЦёЦҪЧҡЦ°
Note: Zechariah is providing background information to help readers understand what happens next in the story. In your translation, introduce this information in a way that would be natural in your own language and culture.
The pronoun **he** refers to the angel of Yahweh. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [And the angel of Yahweh answered and said]
The pronoun **he** refers to the angel of Yahweh, and **him** refers to Joshua. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [And the angel of Yahweh said to Joshua]

Quote: Ч”ЦёвҒ ЧўЦ№ЧһЦ°Ч“ЦҙЦӨЧҷЧқ ЧңЦ°вҒ ЧӨЦёЧ ЦёЧҷвҒ Ч•Цҷ

# Zechariah 4 Introduction

## Structure and Formatting

Zechariah 4 is the fifth of eight night visions. The chapter moves from a prophetic vision (a golden lampstand with seven lamps and two olive trees) to an explanation, then to an oracle declaring that Zerubbabel will complete the temple вҖңnot by might and not by power, but by my Spirit,вҖқ and concludes with the identification of the two olive trees as those set apart to serve the Lord of all the earth.

## Religious and Cultural Concepts in This Chapter

### Temple Furniture as Symbol

The lampstand echoes the tabernacle menorah (Exodus 25:31вҖ“40). Seven lamps and continuous olive oil flowing through golden pipes symbolized GodвҖҷs provision for continuous worship and the SpiritвҖҷs empowering presence.

### Anointing and Divine Empowering

вҖңSons of the fresh oilвҖқ is an idiom for those set apart for special service. Olive oil consecrated kings and priests; here it represents the Spirit enabling Zerubbabel and his community to complete the rebuilding work.

## Translation Issues in This Chapter

### Quotations

Verses 6вҖ“10 contain a series of quotes where it is difficult to discern who is speaking. The ULT suggests:
- the angel speaks from 6вҖ“10
- the angel quotes Yahweh in the middle of verse 6 and beginning of verse 9
- the angel prophetically quotes the peopleвҖҷs reaction at the end of verse 7
- the angel, not Zechariah, is the speaker who received the word of Yahweh in verse 8
- the вҖңmeвҖқ at the end of verse 9 refers to the angel, this is probably the most contested point, scholars are divided as to if this is Zechariah, the angel speaking to him, or the Angel of Yahweh (read as Jesus)
If there is a translation in your area, you may wish to follow itвҖҷs decisions regarding speakers in this section. If not, you may wish to follow the ULT.

### Mountains as Obstacles

Verse 7 calls a вҖңgreat mountainвҖқ to become вҖңa plain,вҖқ representing political and practical obstacles dissolving so that Zerubbabel can set the capstone in place.

# Zechariah 5 Introduction

## Structure and Formatting

This chapter presents two [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/other/vision]]s in which an [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/angel]] interprets what Zechariah sees. The first vision (verses 1вҖ“4) shows a flying [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/other/scroll]] carrying a divine [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/curse]] against thieves and false swearers. The second vision (verses 5вҖ“11) shows [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/evil]] personified as a woman sealed inside an ephah and carried away to [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/names/shinar]].

## Religious and Cultural Concepts in This Chapter

### Personification of Wickedness

The angel identifies the woman inside the ephah as вҖңthe wickedness,вҖқ using a human figure to represent the communityвҖҷs sin. Translators should preserve this identification without reducing it to a mere symbol.

### Ancient Measurements

The chapter refers to cubits (verse 2) and the ephah (verses 6вҖ“10). These measurement units may need explanatory notes for readers unfamiliar with ancient systems.

Zechariah is using the term **behold** to focus his listenersвҖҷ attention on what he is about to say. Your language may have a comparable expression that you can use in your translation. Alternate translation: [pay attention]

Zechariah is using **I lifted up my eyes** as a common expression of the culture to mean вҖңI raised my eyes to look.вҖқ If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [I raised my eyes]

Quote: Ч•ЦёвҒ ЧҗЦёЧ©ЧҒЦ•Ч•ЦјЧ‘ Ч•ЦёвҒ ЧҗЦ¶Ч©ЧӮЦјЦёЦҘЧҗ ЧўЦөЧҷЧ Ц·Ц–вҒ Чҷ Ч•ЦёЦҪвҒ ЧҗЦ¶ЧЁЦ°ЧҗЦ¶Ц‘Ч”

# Zechariah 6 General Notes

## Structure and Formatting

This chapter is written mainly in prose and tells the last vision of the four chariots in the first part of the chapter.

Some translations set each line of poetry farther to the right than the rest of the text to make it easier to read. The ULT does this with the poetic song in 6:12вҖ“13.

## Translation Issues in This Chapter

### Meaning of the visions

The visions are stated and even when the prophet asked for an explanation, their true meaning is often obscure. This uncertainty should remain in your translation. (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/prophet]])
Here the word вҖңeyesвҖқ represents the person who sees. Alternate translation: [looked up]

Quote: between two mountains; and the two mountains were made of bronze
Note: Since the second phrase describes the mountains in the first phrase, the two phrases may be combined. Alternate translation: [between two bronze mountains]

Quote: The first chariot had red horses
Note: It is implicit that the horses were pulling the chariots. Alternate translation: [The first chariot had red horses pulling it] or [Red horses were pulling the first chariot]

Quote: These are the four winds of heaven
Note: The chariots symbolize the four winds of heaven, but they are not literally the four winds. Alternate translation: [These chariots represent the four winds of heaven]
This phrase refers to the four directions from which the wind blows: north, east, south, and west. However, some modern versions interpret this Hebrew expression to mean вҖңfour spirits.вҖқ

Quote: standing before the Lord of all the earth
Note: The idiom to вҖңstand beforeвҖқ means to be in a personвҖҷs presence and to serve him. See how you translated this in [Zechariah 4:14](../04/14.md). Alternate translation: [serving the Lord of all the earth]

Quote: the white horses are going out to the west country
Note: Some modern versions interpret this Hebrew phrase to mean вҖңthe white horses are going out after them,вҖқ that is, following the black horses to the north country.

Quote: the white horses are вҖҰ the spotted gray horses are
Note: Here the horses represent the chariots that they pull. Alternate translation: [the chariot with the white horses is вҖҰ the chariot with the spotted gray horses is]
Alternate translation: [the angel called out to me]
Alternate translation: [Look at the black horses]
These are the names of men.
Alternate translation: [today]
This is the name of a man.

Quote: take the silver and gold, make a crown
Note: Alternate translation: [make a crown with the silver and gold]
Alternate translation: [Speak to Joshua and say]

The word вҖңBranchвҖқ refers to a messianic figure that would serve as king over YahwehвҖҷs people. Yahweh speaks of this person as if he were a branch that grows out from a tree. Since the word вҖңBranchвҖқ is a title, it should be translated with an equivalent word in your language. See how you translated this name in [Zechariah 3:8](../03/08.md).

Quote: He will grow up where he is
Note: The phrase вҖңgrow upвҖқ refers to the growth of a plant, and so continues the metaphor of this person as a вҖңBranch.вҖқ This likely refers to this person appearing or coming into power as the one who would supervise the rebuilding of the temple.

Quote: He will be a priest on his throne

If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [You will give the crown]

This refers to the Israelites who remain in Babylon.
The word вҖңyouвҖқ is plural and refers to the people of Israel.

This chapter starts about two years after the previous chapter and is written in prose.

Some translations prefer to set apart quotations. The ULT and many other English translations set the lines of 7:4вҖ“14, which is an extended quotation, farther to the right on the page than the rest of the text.
вҖңon day 4вҖқ

The assumed knowledge is that the Jews mourned during a part of the seventh month of the Hebrew calendar because in this month the remaining Jews in Jerusalem fled to Egypt after the murder of Gedaliah, whom the king of Babylon appointed as governor over Judah.
The assumed knowledge is that the people of Israel had been slaves in Babylon for 70 years.

Note: This question is used to make the people think about whether they were honoring Yahweh when they ate and drank. If it would be helpful in your language, you could express this question as a statement. Alternate translation: [it was for yourselves that you ate and drank.]

Note: Yahweh uses a question to scold the people. If it would be helpful in your language, you could express this question as a statement. Alternate translation: [These are the same words вҖҰ to the west.]

Note: Here вҖңmouthвҖқ is a metonym for the words spoken by the mouth. Alternate translation: [by the words of the former prophets] or [through the former prophets]

Note: The people refusing to hear and obey Yahweh are compared to their being as unyielding as rock. Alternate translation: [They stubbornly refused to hear the law or the words of Yahweh of hosts]

This chapter is written mainly in prose about the cities and peoples around where Israel stood as a country until the exile.

Some translations set each line of poetry farther to the right than the rest of the text to make it easier to read. The ULT does this with the poetic song in 9:9вҖ“10.

This chapter is written mainly in prose but still uses figurative language as the writer shares a message of redemption and hope for the exiles. (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/redeem]])

Some translations set each line of poetry farther to the right than the rest of the text to make it easier to read. The ULT does this with the poetic song in 10:1вҖ“2.
Alternate translation: [causes plants to grow in the field]

Note: Here вҖңheartsвҖқ refers to the whole person. Alternate translation: [and they will be very happy]

Note: Alternate translation: [their children will see what has taken place and will be happy because of what Yahweh has done for them!]

To whistle is to produce a high, shrill sound with air through narrowed lips. It is often done to give a signal to other people, as here.

# General Information:\n\nIn verses 6вҖ“12, Yahweh is speaking to the people of Israel.

# Zechariah 11 General Notes

## Structure and Formatting

This chapter unlike the previous one is a warning against the leaders amongst the exiles.

Some translations set each line of poetry farther to the right than the rest of the text to make it easier to read. The ULT does this with the poetic song in 11:1вҖ“3, 17.

## Translation Issues in This Chapter

### Metaphor

This chapter uses an extended metaphor of sheep and shepherds to convey YahwehвҖҷs dismay at the leaders during the exiles. (See: [[rc://*/ta/man/translate/figs-metaphor]])

### Symbolism

The writer uses symbolism. Zechariah is told to become a shepherd. He uses two staffs and names them вҖңUnityвҖқ and вҖңFavor.вҖқ He does this very purposefully. In order to preserve this symbolism, it is important to pay attention to the specific words used. (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/favor]])

Quote: Open your doors, Lebanon, that fire may devour
Note: Not resisting what is about to happen is spoken of as if Lebanon were opening its doors. Alternate translation: [People of Lebanon, get ready, because fire will devour]

Fire completely burning up the cedars is spoken of as if the fire would devour the cedars. Alternate translation: [fire may completely destroy your cedars] or [fire may completely burn up your cedars]

Quote: Lament, cypress trees, for the cedar trees have fallen
Note: Cypress trees are spoken of as if they could grieve like a human. Alternate translation: [If the trees were people, they would cry out in sorrow. The cypress trees stand alone because the cedars have burned and fallen]

Quote: What was majestic has been devastated
Note: If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [The majestic cedar trees are no more] or [The cedar trees were once majestic, but now they are ruined]

Quote: Lament, you oaks of Bashan, for the strong forest has gone down.
Note: The oaks of Bash are spoken of as if they could grieve like a human. Alternate translation: [If the oak trees in Bashan were people, they would wail, for their thick forests are gone]
Alternate translation: [The shepherds cry out loudly]

Quote: for their glory has been destroyed
Note: Here вҖңtheir gloryвҖқ probably represents the rich pastures that the shepherds led their sheep to. Alternate translation: [for their rich pastures are ruined]

Quote: for the pride of the Jordan River has been devastated
Note: Here вҖңthe prideвҖқ probably represents the forests that grew near the Jordan River. Alternate translation: [because the trees and shrubbery where they lived by the Jordan River are ruined]

Quote: watch over the flock set aside for slaughter
Note: Alternate translation: [take care of a flock of sheep that the owners plan to slaughter]
If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [no one punishes them]
Alternate translation: [Listen!] or [Pay attention!]
The word вҖңmyselfвҖқ is used to emphasize that it is Yahweh who will do these things.

Quote: turn over every person into the hand of his neighbor and into the hand of his king
Note: Here вҖңhandвҖқ represents power or control. Alternate translation: [allow the people to harm each other and the king will oppress them]

Quote: none of them will I deliver them from their hand
Note: Here вҖңhandвҖқ represents power or control. Alternate translation: [I will not save them from those who are harming them] or [I will not rescue them]

Quote: for those who dealt in sheep
Note: Alternate translation: [for those who bought and sold sheep]
Other versions of the Bible translate this word as вҖңgrace,вҖқ вҖңbeauty,вҖқ or вҖңpleasant.вҖқ
This could mean: (1) вҖңthemвҖқ refers to the three shepherds or (2) вҖңthemвҖқ refers to the sheep owners.

Quote: the sheep that are being destroyedвҖ”let them be destroyed
Note: If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [the sheep that are perishingвҖ”let them perish] or [the sheep that are to wander and get lostвҖ”let them get lost]
If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [the covenant ended]
It is implied that those watching knew Yahweh was giving them a message through the breaking of the staff. Alternate translation: [knew that Yahweh had given them a message]
It is implied that this was very little pay for the shepherd. Alternate translation: [only thirty pieces of silver]
вҖң30 piecesвҖқ

This is the place in the temple of Yahweh where the priests kept the money. Many versions of the Bible translate this as вҖңpotter.вҖқ This is the person who would melt metal to make vessels out of it. Here Yahweh would mean that the payment is so small that the shepherd should have the silver melted to show how insulted he was.
Yahweh uses irony to say that this price was very small for a shepherd doing YahwehвҖҷs work. Alternate translation: [the ridiculously small amount of money]

Here вҖңJudahвҖқ represents the people of the southern kingdom and вҖңIsraelвҖқ represents the people of the northern kingdom. Alternate translation: [between the people of Judah and the people of Israel]

Quote: I am about to set in place a shepherd in the land
Note: Here вҖңset in placeвҖқ is an idiom. Alternate translation: [I am about to appoint a shepherd in the land] or [I am about to put a shepherd in charge in the land]
Alternate translation: [the fattest sheep] or [the best sheep]
This was probably done as an act of cruelty.
Here вҖңswordвҖқ represents enemies who will attack the shepherd. Alternate translation: [May enemies]

Quote: come against his arm and his right eye
Note: Here вҖңcome againstвҖқ is an idiom. Alternate translation: [strike and wound his right arm and pierce his right eye]
Here вҖңarmвҖқ represents the power to fight.
A soldier would use his right eye to look around the shield that he held with his left hand. If his right eye was wounded, he would not be able to see to fight in war.
Alternate translation: [his arm waste away] or [his arm become completely useless]

# Zechariah 12 General Notes

## Structure and Formatting

Similar to chapter 11, this chapter is written mainly in prose and is talking about Jerusalem.

## Religious and Cultural Concepts in This Chapter

### Prophecy

Verses 10вҖ“11 are quoted in the New Testament and apply to JesusвҖҷ death on the cross. (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/cross]])
# General Information:\n\nThese verses begin a section that tells about the coming attack against Jerusalem and how God will rescue the city.
This is a metaphor that speaks of the sky as if it were like a scroll that is rolled up and needs to be stretched out. Alternate translation: [the one who created the sky]

Note: This is a metaphor that speaks of the earth as if it were a building with a foundation. Alternate translation: [put all the earth into place]

Alternate translation: [Soon it will be like I make Jerusalem into a cup вҖҰ to stagger about]
Here вҖңcupвҖқ represents the cup and the contents within the cup. Alternate translation: [into a cup full of wine] or [into a cup full of an alcoholic drink]
Here вҖңherвҖқ refers to the city of Jerusalem. It was common in Hebrew to speak of a city or country as if it were a woman.

Quote: I will make Jerusalem a heavy stone for all the peoples
Note: Yahweh using Jerusalem to punish the surrounding peoples is spoken of as if he would make Jerusalem a heavy stone. Alternate translation: [It will be like I will make Jerusalem a heavy stone for all the peoples]
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue telling about the coming attack against Jerusalem and how God will rescue the city.

Alternate translation: [Yahweh of hosts, the God they worship]

a wooden stick that is burning at one end which gives light as one travels or carries fire somewhere

Quote: on their right and on their left
Note: Here вҖңrightвҖқ and вҖңleftвҖқ represent every direction. Alternate translation: [in every direction]

Note: Here вҖңJerusalemвҖқ represents the people who live there. Alternate translation: [The people of Jerusalem will again live in their own city]
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue telling about the coming attack against Jerusalem and how God will rescue the city.
Here вҖңtentsвҖқ represents homes, and homes represent the people who live in them. Alternate translation: [the people of Judah]
This could mean: (1) the descendants of David or (2) the ruling class of people.

# Zechariah 13 Introduction

## Structure and Formatting

Verses 1вҖ“6 are a prose oracle about cleansing from sin and removing false [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/prophet|prophets]]. Verses 7вҖ“9 shift to poetry, announcing the striking of YahwehвҖҷs [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/other/shepherd|shepherd]] and the [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/purify|purifying]] of a surviving [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/remnant|remnant]].

## Religious and Cultural Concepts in This Chapter

### The Prophetic Cloak

The вҖңcloak of hairвҖқ (verse 4) is the rough garment that identified [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/prophet|prophets]]. False prophets abandon this clothing to conceal their identity.

## Translation Issues in This Chapter

### Nested Quotations

YahwehвҖҷs speech spans the entire chapter and contains embedded quotes from parents (verse 3), a false prophet (verses 5вҖ“6), and a covenant declaration (verse 9).

Here, **the house of David** represents the descendants of David and the royal dynasty. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [the descendants of David]
Zechariah is using the phrase On that day to introduce a new event in the story. Use a word, phrase, or other method in your language that is natural for introducing a new event.

Here Zechariah is speaking as if the means of cleansing from sin were a fountain that would be opened. Your language may have a comparable expression that you can use in your translation, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [a source of cleansing will be made available]

The expression a fountain will be opened is a passive verbal form. If it would be unnatural to use that form, you could express the idea in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. If you need to say who does the action, the context indicates that it is God. Alternate translation: [God will open a fountain]

Quote: ЧңЦ°вҒ Ч—Ц·ЧҳЦјЦ·Ц–ЧҗЧӘ Ч•ЦјвҒ ЧңЦ°вҒ Ч ЦҙЧ“ЦјЦёЦҪЧ”
Note: Zechariah is referring to all types of uncleanness by naming two major components, sin and impurity. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [every kind of sin and uncleanness]

Quote: ЧңЦ°вҒ Ч—Ц·ЧҳЦјЦ·Ц–ЧҗЧӘ Ч•ЦјвҒ ЧңЦ°вҒ Ч ЦҙЧ“ЦјЦёЦҪЧ”
Note: If your language does not use abstract nouns for the ideas of sin and impurity, you could express the same ideas in other ways. Alternate translation: [sinning вҖҰ being unclean]

Here, **the names of the idols** represents the memory and reputation of the idols. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [the memory of the idols]
Yahweh is using the phrase And it will be on that day to introduce a new event in the story. Use a word, phrase, or other method in your language that is natural for introducing a new event.

The phrase the declaration of Yahweh of Armies is a parenthetical statement within YahwehвҖҷs speech that identifies him as the speaker. In your translation, use a natural way of indicating the speaker of a direct quotation in your language. It may not be necessary to represent this phrase explicitly.

The expression they will no longer be remembered is a passive verbal form. If it would be unnatural to use that form, you could express the idea in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [no one will remember them anymore]

Yahweh is using the possessive form to describe a spirit that is characterized by uncleanness. If a speaker of your language would not use the possessive form for this, you could express this idea in another way. Alternate translation: [the unclean spirit]
If your language does not use an abstract noun for the idea of uncleanness, you could express the same idea in another way. Alternate translation: [what is unclean]

Here, the words **And also** add a further action to the one just described. Yahweh will not only remove the idols but will additionally remove the false prophets and the unclean spirit. If the connection between this statement and the previous one is not clear, you may want to use a different connecting word to show how this statement relates to what comes before it. Alternate translation: [Furthermore,]

Here, **in the name of Yahweh** represents the authority of Yahweh. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [with YahwehвҖҷs authority behind you]

Quote: Ч•Ц°вҒ Ч”ЦёЧҷЦёЦ—Ч” ЧӣЦјЦҙЦҪЧҷЦҫЧҷЦҙЧ ЦјЦёЧ‘ЦөЦЈЧҗ ЧҗЦҙЧҷЧ©ЧҒЦ® ЧўЧ•Ц№Ч“Ц’ Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧҗЦёЧһЦ°ЧЁЦЈЧ•Цј ЧҗЦөЦ ЧңЦёЧҷвҒ Ч• ЧҗЦёЧ‘ЦҙЦЁЧҷвҒ Ч• Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧҗЦҙЧһЦјЦӨвҒ Ч•Ц№ ЧҷЦ№ЦҪЧңЦ°Ч“ЦёЧҷвҒ Ч•Цҷ & ЧӘЦҙЦҪЧ—Ц°ЧҷЦ¶Ц”Ч” ЧӣЦјЦҙЦӣЧҷ Ч©ЧҒЦ¶ЦҘЧ§Ц¶ЧЁ Ч“ЦјЦҙЧ‘ЦјЦ·Ц–ЧЁЦ°ЧӘЦјЦё Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ Ч©ЧҒЦөЦЈЧқ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЦ‘Ч” Ч•ЦјвҒ Ч“Ц°Ч§ЦёЧЁЦ»ЦңвҒ Ч”Ч•Цј ЧҗЦёЧ‘ЦҙЦ§ЧҷвҒ Ч”Ч•Цј Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧҗЦҙЧһЦјЦӣвҒ Ч•Ц№ ЧҷЦ№ЧңЦ°Ч“ЦёЦ–ЧҷвҒ Ч• Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ Ч”ЦҙЧ ЦјЦёЧ‘Ц°ЧҗЦҪвҒ Ч•Ц№
Note: The word translated as for indicates that what follows is a reason for what came before. Use a connector in your language that makes it clear that what follows is a reason for what came before. Alternate translation: [because you have spoken falsehood in the name of Yahweh]
Yahweh is using the phrase And it will be to introduce a new event in the story. Use a word, phrase, or other method in your language that is natural for introducing a new event.

Yahweh is presenting a possible condition, that the parents will condemn and kill their son only if he still prophesies falsely. Use a natural form in your language for introducing a condition that could happen. It may be helpful to reorder this sentence so that the conditional statement is first. Alternate translation: [should a man still prophesy]

Yahweh is not referring to a specific man. He means any person who prophesies falsely in general. If it would be helpful to your readers, you could use an expression that would be more natural in your language. Alternate translation: [anyone who]

Quote: ЧӣЦјЦҙЦҪЧҷ & ЧӘЦҙЦҪЧ—Ц°ЧҷЦ¶Ц”Ч” & Ч©ЧҒЦ¶ЦҘЧ§Ц¶ЧЁ Ч“ЦјЦҙЧ‘ЦјЦ·Ц–ЧЁЦ°ЧӘЦјЦё Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ Ч©ЧҒЦөЦЈЧқ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЦ‘Ч”
Note: If it would be clearer in your language, you could translate this so that there is not a quotation within a quotation. Alternate translation: [that he must not live, because he has spoken falsehood in the name of Yahweh]

The parents of the false prophet are emphasizing a positive meaning by using a negative word, not, together with a mild expression that is the opposite of the intended meaning. They mean that the false prophet will certainly die. If it would be helpful in your language, you could emphasize the positive meaning in a strong way. Alternate translation: [you will certainly die]
If your language does not use an abstract noun for the idea of falsehood, you could express the same idea in another way. Alternate translation: [falsely]

Quote: Ч•Ц°вҒ Ч”ЦёЧҷЦёЦЈЧ” & Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч”Ц—Ч•ЦјЧҗ ЧҷЦөЧ‘Ц№Ц§Ч©ЧҒЧ•Цј Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч ЦјЦ°Ч‘ЦҙЧҷЧҗЦҙЦӣЧҷЧқ ЧҗЦҙЦҘЧҷЧ©ЧҒ ЧһЦөвҒ Ч—Ц¶Ч–Ц°ЧҷЦ№Ч Ц–вҒ Ч•Ц№ Ч‘ЦјЦ°вҒ Ч”ЦҙЧ ЦјЦёЦҪЧ‘Ц°ЧҗЦ№ЧӘЦ‘вҒ Ч•Ц№ Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧңЦ№Ц§Чҗ ЧҷЦҙЧңЦ°Ч‘ЦјЦ°Ч©ЧҒЦӣЧ•Цј ЧҗЦ·Ч“ЦјЦ¶ЦҘЧЁЦ¶ЧӘ Ч©ЧӮЦөЧўЦёЦ–ЧЁ ЧңЦ°ЧһЦ·ЦҘЧўЦ·Чҹ ЧӣЦјЦ·Ч—ЦөЦҪЧ©ЧҒ
Note: Here, **in order to** marks **to deceive** as the goal or purpose of the false prophets putting on a cloak of hair. Use a connector in your language that makes it clear that this is the purpose.
See how you translated the similar expression in [13:2](../13/02.md).

Zechariah is using the possessive form to describe a cloak that is made of hair. If a speaker of your language would not use the possessive form for this, you could express this idea in another way. Alternate translation: [a cloak made of hair]
The pronoun he refers to the false prophet described in the previous verse. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [And the false prophet will say]
If your language does not use an abstract noun for the idea of youth, you could express the same idea in another way. Alternate translation: [when I was young]

Quote: Ч•Ц°вҒ ЧҗЦёЧһЦ·Ц•ЧЁ ЧңЦ№ЦҘЧҗ Ч ЦёЧ‘ЦҙЦ–ЧҷЧҗ ЧҗЦёЧ Ц№Ц‘ЧӣЦҙЧҷ ЧҗЦҙЧҷЧ©ЧҒЦҫЧўЦ№Ч‘ЦөЦӨЧ“ ЧҗЦІЧ“ЦёЧһЦёЧ”Цҷ ЧҗЦёЧ Ц№Ц”ЧӣЦҙЧҷ ЧӣЦјЦҙЦҘЧҷ ЧҗЦёЧ“ЦёЦ–Чқ Ч”ЦҙЧ§Ц°Ч Ц·ЦҘвҒ Ч ЦҙЧҷ ЧһЦҙвҒ Ч ЦјЦ°ЧўЧ•ЦјЧЁЦёЦҪвҒ Чҷ
Note: The word translated as for indicates that what follows is a reason for what came before. Use a connector in your language that makes it clear that what follows is a reason for what came before. Alternate translation: [And he will say, вҖҳI {am} not a prophet. I {am} a man working the ground. The reason is that a man acquired me from my youth.вҖҷ]

Quote: Ч ЦёЧ‘ЦҙЦ–ЧҷЧҗ ЧҗЦёЧ Ц№Ц‘ЧӣЦҙЧҷ ЧҗЦҙЧҷЧ©ЧҒЦҫЧўЦ№Ч‘ЦөЦӨЧ“ ЧҗЦІЧ“ЦёЧһЦёЧ”Цҷ & ЧӣЦјЦҙЦҘЧҷ ЧҗЦёЧ“ЦёЦ–Чқ Ч”ЦҙЧ§Ц°Ч Ц·ЦҘвҒ Ч ЦҙЧҷ ЧһЦҙвҒ Ч ЦјЦ°ЧўЧ•ЦјЧЁЦёЦҪвҒ Чҷ
Note: If it would be clearer in your language, you could translate this so that there is not a quotation within a quotation. Alternate translation: [he is not a prophet but a man working the ground, for a man acquired him from his youth]

The false prophet is using between your hands as a common expression of the culture to mean вҖңon your chestвҖқ or вҖңon your body.вҖқ If this phrase does not have that meaning for your readers, you could use a comparable expression from your language that does have that meaning, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [on your chest]

If it would be clearer in your language, you could translate this so that there is not a quotation within a quotation. Alternate translation: [that those wounds came from being struck in the house of those who loved him]

The expression I was struck is a passive verbal form. If it would be unnatural to use that form, you could express the idea in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [they struck me]

The false prophet is using the participle the ones loving me as a noun to mean people who love him. Your language may use adjectives in the same way. If not, you can translate this with an equivalent phrase. Alternate translation: [those who love me]

Quote: ЧһЦёЦ§Ч” & Ч”ЦёвҒ ЧҗЦөЦ–ЧңЦјЦ¶Ч” Ч‘ЦјЦөЦЈЧҷЧҹ ЧҷЦёЧ“Ц¶Ц‘ЧҷвҒ ЧҡЦё
Note: If it would be clearer in your language, you could translate this so that there is not a quotation within a quotation. Alternate translation: [why he has those wounds between his hands]

The word someone is an indefinite subject that does not refer to a specific person. It refers to anyone who might ask the false prophet this question. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [And if anyone asks him]

Here, **the house of the ones loving me** represents the home or dwelling place of the people who love the speaker. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [the dwelling place of those who love me]

Yahweh is speaking to a **sword** that he knows cannot hear him. He is doing this to communicate to the people listening to him that he feels very strongly about the judgment he is about to bring. If it would be helpful in your language, you could translate these words as if he were speaking directly to the people about the sword. Alternate translation: [Let the sword awake against my shepherd]

Here Yahweh is speaking as if the leader he appointed were **my shepherd**. Your language may have a comparable expression that you can use in your translation, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [the leader I care for]

The phrase **the declaration of Yahweh of Armies** is a parenthetical statement within YahwehвҖҷs speech that identifies him as the speaker. In your translation, use a natural way of indicating the speaker of a direct quotation in your language. It may not be necessary to represent this phrase explicitly.

Here Yahweh is speaking as if the people were **the flock** that **will be scattered**. Your language may have a comparable expression that you can use in your translation, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [the people will scatter]

Here, **my hand** represents YahwehвҖҷs power and action. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [I will direct my power against]

Yahweh is using the adjective **little** as a noun to mean the weak and vulnerable people. Your language may use adjectives in the same way. If not, you can translate this adjective with an equivalent phrase. Alternate translation: [the weak and vulnerable ones]
Yahweh is using the phrase **And it will be** to introduce a new event in the story. Use a word, phrase, or other method in your language that is natural for introducing a new event.

The phrase **the declaration of Yahweh** is a parenthetical statement within YahwehвҖҷs speech that identifies him as the speaker. In your translation, use a natural way of indicating the speaker of a direct quotation in your language. It may not be necessary to represent this phrase explicitly.

The expression **two parts in it will be cut off** is a passive verbal form. If it would be unnatural to use that form, you could express the idea in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. If you need to say who does the action, the context indicates that it is Yahweh. Alternate translation: [Yahweh will cut off two parts in it]

Quote: Ч‘ЦјЦёЦ”вҒ Ч”Цј & Ч•Ц°вҒ Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч©ЧҒЦјЦ°ЧңЦҙЧ©ЧҒЦҙЦ–ЧҷЧӘ ЧҷЦҙЧ•ЦјЦёЦҘЧӘЦ¶ЧЁ
Note: The expression **the third will be left in it** is a passive verbal form. If it would be unnatural to use that form, you could express the idea in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. If you need to say who does the action, the context indicates that it is Yahweh. Alternate translation: [Yahweh will leave the third in it]

The expressions **will be cut off** and **will expire** mean similar things. Yahweh is using the two terms together for emphasis. If it would be clearer for your readers, you could express the emphasis with a single phrase. Alternate translation: [will perish completely]
Here, **two parts** means two parts out of three equal parts, and **the third** means one part out of three equal parts.

Quote: Ч•Ц°вҒ Ч”ЦөЧ‘ЦөЧҗЧӘЦҙЦӨЧҷ Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч©ЧҒЦјЦ°ЧңЦҙЧ©ЧҒЦҙЧҷЧӘЦҷ Ч‘ЦјЦёвҒ ЧҗЦөЦ”Ч©ЧҒ
Note: Here Yahweh is speaking as if the trials and suffering he would bring upon the people were **the fire**. Your language may have a comparable expression that you can use in your translation, or you could state the meaning plainly. Alternate translation: [I will bring the third through great suffering]

The pronoun **He** refers to the remaining third of the people, spoken of collectively as a single group. It may be helpful to clarify this for your readers. Alternate translation: [That remaining third will call on my name]
Here, **my name** represents Yahweh himself. If it would be helpful in your language, you could use an equivalent expression or plain language. Alternate translation: [me]

The speaker is not using this possessive form **my God** to indicate that he possesses or controls Yahweh. Rather, he is using these words to indicate that Yahweh is the God whom he worships. If a speaker of your language would not use the possessive form for this, you could express this idea in another way. Alternate translation: [the God I worship]

Quote: Ч•ЦјвҒ ЧҰЦ°ЧЁЦ·ЧӨЦ°ЧӘЦјЦҙЧҷвҒ ЧқЦҷ ЧӣЦјЦҙвҒ ЧҰЦ°ЧЁЦ№ЦЈЧЈ Ч”Ц·вҒ ЧӣЦјЦ¶Ц”ЧЎЦ¶ЧЈ Ч•ЦјвҒ Ч‘Ц°Ч—Ц·Ч Ц°ЧӘЦјЦҙЦ–ЧҷвҒ Чқ ЧӣЦјЦҙвҒ Ч‘Ц°Ч—Ц№ЦЈЧҹ Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч–ЦјЦёЧ”ЦёЦ‘Ч‘
Note: These two phrases **I will refine them as one refines the silver** and **I will test them as one tests the gold** mean basically the same thing. The second emphasizes the meaning of the first by repeating the same idea with different words. If it would be helpful to your readers, you could combine them and express the emphasis in another way. Alternate translation: [I will thoroughly purify and test them as a metalworker refines silver and gold]

Quote: Ч”ЦЈЧ•ЦјЧҗ & ЧҗЦёЧһЦ·ЦЁЧЁЦ°ЧӘЦјЦҙЧҷЦҷ ЧўЦ·ЧһЦјЦҙЦЈвҒ Чҷ & Ч•Ц°вҒ Ч”ЦҘЧ•ЦјЧҗ ЧҷЦ№ЧҗЧһЦ·Ц–ЧЁ ЧҷЦ°Ч”Ч•ЦёЦҘЧ” ЧҗЦұЧңЦ№Ч”ЦёЦҪвҒ Чҷ
Note: If it would be clearer in your language, you could translate this so that there is not a quotation within a quotation. Alternate translation: [I will say that he is my people, and he will say that Yahweh is his God.]

Quote: Ч•ЦјвҒ ЧҰЦ°ЧЁЦ·ЧӨЦ°ЧӘЦјЦҙЧҷвҒ ЧқЦҷ ЧӣЦјЦҙвҒ ЧҰЦ°ЧЁЦ№ЦЈЧЈ Ч”Ц·вҒ ЧӣЦјЦ¶Ц”ЧЎЦ¶ЧЈ
Note: The point of this comparison is that just as **silver** is refined by fire to remove impurities, so Yahweh will purify his people through trials. If it would be helpful in your language, you could make this point explicitly. Alternate translation: [I will purify them of impurities, just as fire purifies silver]

Quote: Ч•ЦјвҒ Ч‘Ц°Ч—Ц·Ч Ц°ЧӘЦјЦҙЦ–ЧҷвҒ Чқ ЧӣЦјЦҙвҒ Ч‘Ц°Ч—Ц№ЦЈЧҹ Ч”Ц·вҒ Ч–ЦјЦёЧ”ЦёЦ‘Ч‘
Note: The point of this comparison is that just as **gold** is tested by fire to prove its genuineness, so Yahweh will test his people through trials. If it would be helpful in your language, you could make this point explicitly. Alternate translation: [I will test them to prove they are genuine, just as gold is tested by fire to prove its genuineness]

# Zechariah 14 General Notes

## Structure and Formatting

This chapter is written in prose about Jerusalem during the last days. (See: [[rc://*/tw/dict/bible/kt/lastday]])

## Religious and Cultural Concepts in This Chapter

### Last days

Zechariah often speaks of the last days by using the phrase вҖңthat dayвҖқ or вҖңin that day.вҖқ When referencing a future вҖңday,вҖқ the translator should be aware that it is possible that the author is speaking about the last days.
# General Information:\n\nThis chapter describes the final war for the city of Jerusalem and how God will save it.

Quote: A day for Yahweh is coming when your plunder will be divided in your midst
Note: A future time is spoken of as if вҖңa day is coming.вҖқ The phrase вҖңwill be dividedвҖқ can be stated in active form. Alternate translation: [Soon Yahweh will judge you, and he will allow your enemies to take all of your possessions and divide if for themselves while you watch] (See also: [[rc://*/ta/man/translate/figs-activepassive]])

Quote: I will gather every nation against Jerusalem for battle
Note: Here вҖңevery nationвҖқ is a generalization that means вҖңmany nations.вҖқ Alternate translation: [I will cause many nations to attack Jerusalem]

If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [your enemies will capture the city]

Quote: The houses will be plundered and the women raped
Note: If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [Enemies will plunder the houses and rape the women]

Quote: the remainder of the people will not be cut off from the city
Note: Not removing people from the city is spoken of as if the people will not be вҖңcut off.вҖқ If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [your enemies will allow the remaining people to stay in the city]

# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save it. In this prophecy, Yahweh is described as a warrior who will come and fight in battle.

Quote: as when he wages war on the day of battle
Note: Alternate translation: [just as he fought battles in the past]
Alternate translation: [At that time]

Quote: his feet will stand on the Mount of Olives
Note: Here вҖңfeetвҖқ represents Yahweh. Alternate translation: [he will stand on the Mount of Olives]

Quote: The Mount of Olives will be split вҖҰ by a very great valley
Note: If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [The presences of Yahweh will split the Mount of Olives вҖҰ causing there to be a very great valley]
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save her.
Here вҖңyouвҖқ is plural and refers to the people of Jerusalem.
This refers to the mountains created after the Mount of Olives split in half.
This is the name of a town or village east of Jerusalem.

Quote: You will flee just as you fled
Note: Here вҖңYou willвҖқ refers to the people of Jerusalem. But, вҖңyou fledвҖқ refers to their ancestors since this describes an event that happened many years earlier. Alternate translation: [You will flee just as your ancestors fled]

Quote: in the days of Uzziah, king of Judah
Note: Here вҖңin the daysвҖқ is an idiom that refers to the time when Uzziah was king. Alternate translation: [when Uzziah was king of Judah]
This probably refers to GodвҖҷs angels.
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save her.
Alternate translation: [At that time]
It is implied that there will be no light from the sun. Alternate translation: [there will be no light from the sun]

Quote: a day known only to Yahweh
Note: If your language does not use the passive form in this way, you can state this in active form or in another way that is natural in your language. Alternate translation: [only Yahweh knows when that day will begin] or [only Yahweh knows when that time will begin]
This normally means running or flowing water, rather than still or stagnant water.
This refers to the Dead Sea, which is east of Jerusalem.
This refers to the Mediterranean Sea.
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save her.

Quote: there will be Yahweh, the one God, and his name alone
Note: Here вҖңnameвҖқ represents YahwehвҖҷs reputation or character. Alternate translation: [people will know that Yahweh is the only true God] or [people will only worship Yahweh, the one true God]
It is implied that this is the land of Judah. Alternate translation: [All the land of Judah]
This is the name of a plain in the Jordan River Valley.
This is the name of a town on the northern border of Judah.
This is the name of a town south of Jerusalem.

Quote: Jerusalem will continue to be raised up
Note: This contrasts Jerusalem, which will remain at a higher elevation, to the surrounding area, which is lower in elevation. The contrast can be indicated by adding the word вҖңbut.вҖқ This can also be stated in active form. Alternate translation: [But, Jerusalem will remain high up] (See also: [[rc://*/ta/man/translate/figs-activepassive]])

Quote: the Benjamin Gate вҖҰ the first gate вҖҰ the Corner Gate
Note: These are names of gates in the northeast part of city wall of Jerusalem. This could mean: (1) вҖңthe first gateвҖқ and вҖңthe Corner GateвҖқ refer to the same gate or (2) вҖңthe first gateвҖқ and вҖңthe Corner gateвҖқ are separate gates. (See also: [[rc://*/ta/man/translate/figs-explicit]])
This refers to a strong point in the city defenses on the northern wall. It was probably built by a man named Hananel.
This probably refers to the place where wine was made for the royal family. It was located in the southwest part of Jerusalem.
Here вҖңJerusalemвҖқ represents the people. Alternate translation: [The people will live safely in Jerusalem]
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save her.

Quote: even as they are standing on their feet
Note: вҖңwhile they are still standing up.вҖқ This emphasizes how quickly their flesh will rot away. They will not even have time to lie down.

Quote: that great fear from Yahweh will come among them
Note: If your language does not use an abstract noun for the idea behind the word **fear**, you can express the same idea with a verbal form such as вҖңterrified.вҖқ Alternate translation: [Yahweh will cause the people to be very terrified]

Quote: Each one will seize the hand of another, and the hand of one will be raised up against the hand of another
Note: These are idioms that refer to being hostile towards another person. Alternate translation: [Each person will grab someone, and they will fight each other]
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save her.

Quote: Judah will also fight against Jerusalem
Note: Here вҖңJudahвҖқ and вҖңJerusalemвҖқ represent the people that live there. Alternate translation: [Even the other people in Judah will fight against the people of Jerusalem]

Quote: Judah will also fight against Jerusalem
Note: Some versions of the Bible read, вҖңJudah will also fight at Jerusalem.вҖқ
Alternate translation: [They will capture all the valuable possessions]
Alternate translation: [in great quantities]
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save her.
Here вҖңcame againstвҖқ is an idiom. Alternate translation: [that attacked Jerusalem]

Quote: will instead go up from year to year
Note: Alternate translation: [will instead go to Jerusalem every year]
Alternate translation: [the Festival of Tabernacles] or [the Festival of Booths] or [the Festival of Tents]

Quote: A plague from Yahweh will attack the nations
Note: Causing people to suffer from a plague is spoken of as if the plague would attack the people like an army. Alternate translation: [Yahweh will cause a plague among the people of the nations]
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save her.
# General Information:\n\nThese verses continue the description of the final war for the city of Jerusalem and of how God will save her.

Quote: the bells of the horses will say
Note: Alternate translation: [the inscription on the bells of the horses will say]
These basins were used for boiling meat in the courtyard of the temple. Alternate translation: [the cooking pots in the courtyard of the temple]

Quote: will be like the bowls before the altar
Note: It is implied that the basins will be sacred like the bowls used for catching the blood of the sacrifices. Alternate translation: [will be as sacred as the bowls used at the altar]

Quote: every pot in Jerusalem and Judah will be set apart to Yahweh of hosts
Note: Various types of pots and utensils were made especially to be used in the temple for the worship of Yahweh and for the sacrifices. These were considered special, not to be used for anything else.

Quote: traders will no longer be in the house of Yahweh
Note: It was the custom for traders to sell the people things they needed in order to make proper sacrifices to Yahweh in the temple. Alternate translation: [people will no longer buy or sell things in the courtyard of the temple of Yahweh]
Some versions of the Bible translate вҖңtradersвҖқ as вҖңCanaanites.вҖқ
